required_skills: Site Reliability Engineering Principles and SRE Culture
Service Level Objectives and Error Budget Management
Observability with Metrics, Logs, and Traces
Automation of Toil using Python or Go
preferred_skills: Chaos Engineering and Failure Mode Analysis
Disaster Recovery Testing and Game Days
Progressive Delivery and Canary Release Management
responsibilities: Define, instrument, and enforce Service Level Objectives and error budgets for critical production services in collaboration with product and engineering teams
Lead incident response for high-severity production outages, coordinating cross-team war rooms, driving root cause analysis, and publishing blameless post-mortems
Build automated toil-elimination tooling in Python or Go to replace manual operational tasks with reliable, monitored, self-healing system behaviors
Design and maintain a unified observability platform integrating Prometheus metrics, structured logging, and distributed traces for full production visibility
Conduct capacity planning exercises by analyzing traffic growth trends and provisioning infrastructure headroom to prevent saturation before it impacts users
Implement and validate disaster recovery procedures including multi-region failover, backup restoration drills, and RTO/RPO verification under simulated failure conditions
Configure intelligent alerting rules in Alertmanager and PagerDuty that minimize false positives while ensuring on-call engineers are paged for actionable incidents
Partner with software engineering teams during design reviews to identify reliability risks and prescribe SRE best practices including circuit breakers and graceful degradation
Run chaos engineering experiments using controlled fault injection to expose hidden failure modes and validate system resilience before they manifest in production
Drive adoption of progressive delivery strategies such as canary deployments and feature flags to reduce blast radius of new releases across production traffic
